articles.headline: US Army orders 2,500 Skydio drones in record deal
articles.blurb: The US Army just made one thing very clear: small drones aren’t just helpful anymore; they’re essential. articles.takeaway: The $52M Army contract signals that domestic drone manufacturers can now compete at scale with established defense suppliers, potentially reshaping US military procurement patterns.

articles.headline: SES targets 28 satellites with K2 Space for next-gen MEO network

articles.takeaway: SES's partnership with K2 Space signals a shift toward outsourced satellite manufacturing for next-gen MEO constellations, reducing development risk through modular design approaches.

articles.headline: Why Airlines Are Retiring the 757
articles.blurb: Few aircraft in commercial aviation have earned the kind of reputation the Boeing 757 built over the years. articles.takeaway: Airlines are retiring the 757 not due to capability gaps but because its fuel efficiency and operating costs can no longer compete with modern narrowbody aircraft like the A321.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across all aviation layers—from individual flights to airport systems—offers near-term emissions and efficiency gains without waiting for zero-emission aircraft maturity.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts
